title: sarothamnus scoparius
intro: Sarothamnus scoparius, also known as broom or Scotch broom, is a flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The health benefits of broom include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as a natural diuretic. Broom can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health and reduce menstrual cramps.
